description: Create a UML sequence diagram in Wondershare EdrawMax depicting a payment processing flow titled 'Payment Processing Sequence Diagram'. The diagram should include six participants arranged left-to-right: 'User', 'Web Frontend', 'API Gateway', 'Payment Service', 'Fraud Detection', and 'Bank API'. Document the following sequence of messages: 'User' sends 'Submit Payment' (Synchronous) to 'Web Frontend'; 'Web Frontend' sends 'POST /payments' (Synchronous) to 'API Gateway'; 'API Gateway' sends 'processPayment()' (Synchronous) to 'Payment Service'; 'Payment Service' sends 'checkFraud()' (Synchronous) to 'Fraud Detection'; 'Fraud Detection' returns 'fraudResult()' to 'Payment Service'; 'Payment Service' sends 'authorizeTransaction()' (Synchronous) to 'Bank API'; 'Bank API' returns 'authorizationResponse()' to 'Payment Service'; 'Payment Service' returns 'paymentResult()' to 'API Gateway'; 'API Gateway' returns 'HTTP 200 Response' to 'Web Frontend'; and 'Web Frontend' returns 'Payment Confirmation' to 'User'. Save the editable diagram to '/home/ga/Documents/payment_sequence_diagram.eddx' and export it as an image to '/home/ga/Documents/payment_sequence_diagram.png'. If an account login dialog appears on startup, dismiss it.
